# A Treatise Concerning the Principles of Human Knowledge

*[George Berkeley](../philosophers/berkeley.md) · 1710*

The major statement of Berkeley's idealism, which radicalises empiricism into its famous thesis, **esse est percipi**, to be is to be perceived. He there denies the existence of any mind-independent matter, bodies being but collections of sensible ideas.
